title: Packet 067 And Packet 068 Authority Publication And Host Mirror Resync Gate
objective: Publish Packet 067 local closeout authority and Packet 068 post-publication readiness decision authority through the parent-root boundary, then restore `/home/olares/code/apex` to clean parity without treating authority publication as source/test execution, migration approval, simultaneous multi-worker approval, or second-worker authority.
dependency_note: Packet 067 published the Packet 063 source/test artifact in commit `43635c030e9e16d37eb8c815974e1131fa4193ec`; Packet 068 recorded one full one-mutation-worker pilot cycle complete while keeping simultaneous multi-worker execution, migration, second-worker execution, package/toolchain repair, runtime/service mutation, and old-clone mutation closed.
excluded_drift: Packet 069 local closeout authority drift after publication
any unrelated authority drift outside Packet 067 and Packet 068 closure surfaces
validation_steps: Confirm `/home/olares/src/apex-power-ops-platform` branch, commit, and status count without mutation.
Stage only Packet 067 closeout authority, Packet 068 decision authority, Packet 069 draft authority, routing, and roadmap updates required by this gate.
Run JSON parse checks and `git diff --cached --check` before committing.
Confirm no source, package, or lockfile paths are staged.
Commit and push the bounded authority publication.
Fast-forward `/home/olares/code/apex` non-destructively to the published commit and confirm status count 0.
preserved_boundaries: Authority publication is not source/test execution authority.
One completed one-worker pilot is not simultaneous multi-worker approval.
No second mutation worker opens in Packet 069.
Installs and package-manager activation/download remain closed.
sql_permission_note: This packet authorizes no database writes and no source edits, source/test execution, migration approval, runtime mutation, service mutation, package mutation, lockfile mutation, install, package-manager activation/download, remote rewrite, AI-services expansion, Gitea/code-hosting transition, canonical-hosting transition, second-worker execution, slice widening, rollback, force/reset/clean, or old-clone mutation.
